patch.value: GH-558: export canonical item-to-context-relevance candidate derivation from the public SDK
Promote the existing CLI-owned item signal derivation into a stable public SDK primitive so extensions reproduce pm next/context ranking without deep imports, subprocesses, or drift.

Public SDK derives ContextRelevanceCandidate values from item metadata with the same signals and ordering as pm next/context; CLI consumers use the SDK primitive rather than private logic; browser-safe stable types and deterministic now/author/semantic-score options are documented; focused SDK, CLI parity, barrel, packed-consumer, and exact coverage tests pass
Give every extension the same canonical relevance inputs as pm next and pm context
Eliminates contradictory rankings and subprocess workarounds across packages
Makes context ranking extensible, deterministic, and SDK-first
One exported derivation primitive shared by CLI, packages, and embedded hosts
GitHub #558 demonstrates published extensions cannot reproduce built-in ranking from public APIs

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-14: all-status searches for ContextRelevanceCandidate, signal builder, and GH-558 found the broad pm-3hps feature store and shipped scorer pm-h3no, but no item owns promotion of the existing CLI builder; this child records GitHub #558 without duplicating the larger index-backed store.

author: codex-root
message: Broad SDK relationship persistence, CLI/MCP graph context, conformance, and canonical relevance derivation tranche

value.author: codex-root
value.text: GitHub provenance: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/558 documents the missing public signal builder and the pm-brief subprocess workaround. Canonical implementation lineage is pm-3hps -> pm-4k6b with shipped scorer contract pm-h3no.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: TDD progress: public SDK now owns canonical item-to-relevance signal derivation; context and next are thin consumers. Focused SDK/CLI parity tests pass, including custom status, author affinity, deadline/risk/knowledge pressure, usage affinity, malformed timestamps, and deterministic ties.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Verification evidence: exact full coverage passed 287 files / 5,687 tests at 100% statements (36,748/36,748), branches (26,382/26,382), functions (7,421/7,421), and lines (35,873/35,873). Packed @unbrained/pm-cli@2026.7.14 into /tmp, installed it into a clean npm consumer, and exercised the public buildItemContextRelevanceCandidates export successfully; npm audit reported zero vulnerabilities.

patch.value: Completed GH-558 by exporting deterministic canonical item-to-context-relevance candidate derivation from the public SDK, migrating pm context and pm next to the shared primitive, documenting the contract, and proving SDK/CLI parity plus packed-package consumption with exact repo-wide 100/100/100/100 coverage.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Public buildItemContextRelevanceCandidates is exported and shared by pm context and pm next with documented deterministic metadata signals.

SDK, CLI parity, exact coverage, linked-test, and packed-consumer proofs all pass.

value.text: Pre-commit acceptance review found the public options omitted caller-derived semantic_similarity despite the canonical signal and item acceptance requiring a semantic-score option. Reopened only this item to add the typed map, parity coverage, and documentation before publishing.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Acceptance correction evidence: options now accept caller-derived semanticSimilarity and emit canonical semantic_similarity values. Focused 16-test parity, typecheck, lint, zero clones, static/docstring, package boundary, token budget, full 287-file/5,687-test exact 100/100/100/100 coverage, and a freshly repacked installed consumer asserting semantic_similarity=0.95 all pass.

patch.value: Completed GH-558 with a public deterministic item relevance builder shared by pm context and pm next, including clock, author, usage-affinity, and semantic-similarity inputs; documented and proven through CLI parity, linked tests, fresh packed-package consumption, and exact repo-wide 100/100/100/100 coverage.
